_corpus_meta.narrative: HighKongP95Latency fired on the Kong gateway. The LLM produced an RCA that named memory pressure as the cause and recommended OOMKill remediations (kubectl set resources, JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS, kubectl rollout restart). This is wrong-archetype: a p95 latency alert should not be answered with memory remediations. The pre-S3-HF-09 4-axis scorer rated this RCA acceptably (it had a named cause, state-changing actions, specific evidence). Axis 5 — archetype_match — is the regression guard that would have caught it. If this corpus entry ever scores above F, axis 5 has regressed and the firewall is broken.

rca_report: Kong is hitting JVM heap pressure: container_memory_working_set_bytes peaked near the cgroup limit and we are seeing repeated OOM-kill cycles every ~12 minutes on the spring-boot upstream. The JVM heap defaults to ~25% of the cgroup so a 1GiB container only has ~256MB effective heap which is exhausted under normal traffic. GC overhead climbs in the 60-90s before each crash. The pattern post-dates the most recent deploy.
evidence: ["container_memory_working_set_bytes peaked at 940/1024 MB (~92%) twice in 30m", "kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total = 14 (+3 in last 30m)", "3x java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space (Drain3 cluster #142)", "GC pause sum climbing in the 90s before each OOM"]
suggested_actions: ["kubectl set resources deploy/spring-boot -n app --limits=memory=2Gi --requests=memory=1Gi", "kubectl set env deploy/spring-boot -n app JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS='-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75 -XX:+ExitOnOutOfMemoryError'", "kubectl rollout restart deploy/spring-boot -n app"]
